case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit
party: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd (AT) – Suit No 477 of 2012 (AT (Carolyn))

audit_suggestion.case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit
audit_suggestion.party: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd (AT) – Suit No 477 of 2012 (AT (Carolyn))

fact_changes.evidence: The source states only that Suit No 477 of 2012 is brought by AT against Linda and that it is a derivative action; it does not give a specific date for the suit's commencement in the text provided.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the year only; do not invent a specific date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source describes Suit No 477 of 2012 as a derivative action brought by Carolyn Fong, a shareholder of AT, on behalf of AT against Linda, but does not specify a date for this fact.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the year only; do not invent a specific date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says the High Court in Originating Summons No 505 of 2010 found that PF was the controlling mind and will of AT at the material times, and cites Fong Wai Lyn Carolyn v Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd [2011] 3 SLR 980. The fact as written is broadly supported, but the date should not be presented as the date of the leave grant unless tied to the cited 2011 decision.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Clarify that this refers to the 2011 cited decision, not the originating summons itself.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that Carolyn obtained leave under section 216A of the Companies Act in Originating Summons No 505 of 2010, but does not specify a day or month.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the year only; do not invent a specific date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says different law firms had been engaged to represent AT in the two Suits, but does not specify a date.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the year only; do not invent a specific date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that PF had transferred the majority of his shares to the Fong Foundation charitable trust in 2006, but does not specify a month or day.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the year only; do not invent a specific date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source expressly states that PF passed away on 25 April 2008.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the exact date as stated in the source.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that at the date of PF's death, he held 3.6% of the shares, the Fong Foundation held about 51%, and the remaining shares were held by 11 other persons including Carolyn with 6.2%.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Tie the shareholding figures to the date of PF's death, 25 April 2008, rather than using an approximate 2008 date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that the appeals were heard before the judge on 2 September 2013.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the exact date as stated in the source.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that further submissions were heard on 11 October 2013.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the exact date as stated in the source.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that the application came before the judge on 4 November 2013.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the exact date as stated in the source.

fact_changes.current_value: Whether, in Suit No 477 of 2012, losses to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd from specified transactions can be allocated solely to the conduct of former managing director Linda Kao Chai-Chau or also to conduct attributable to Peter Fong such that contribution or indemnity between them is in issue.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Whether Linda Kao Chai-Chau had authority from Peter Fong to act as she did in relation to the transactions complained of.
fact_changes.evidence: The source describes the dispute as arising from Linda's defence that the transactions were carried out pursuant to PF's instructions and the question whether she could seek contribution or indemnity from PF's estate. The current issue wording incorporates outcome-oriented allocation of losses and contribution framing that is tied to the litigation analysis.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace with a neutral pre-outcome issue statement.

fact_changes.current_value: Where multiple persons are each legally responsible in respect of the same damage suffered by a company, contribution between them may be available under statutory contribution regimes, and procedural rules allow a defendant to bring third party proceedings for contribution or indemnity connected with the original subject matter of the action.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Procedural rules allow a defendant to bring third party proceedings for contribution or indemnity connected with the original subject matter of the action.
fact_changes.evidence: The source includes the procedural rule in O 16 r 1(1) of the Rules of Court. The broader statement about statutory contribution regimes is legal reasoning from the judgment and should not be retained as background fact.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Trim to the neutral procedural rule only.

fact_changes.current_value: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, through the derivative action in Suit No 477 of 2012, seeks recovery for losses said to arise from specified transactions during the period when Linda Kao Chai-Chau was managing director and Peter Fong was Chairman and a significant shareholder. Linda Kao Chai-Chau seeks to involve the estate of Peter Fong in the same proceedings for potential contribution or indemnity in respect of any overlapping loss to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, using the third party procedure provided by the Rules of Court and the statutory contribution framework.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Suit No 477 of 2012 is a derivative action brought by Carolyn Fong on behalf of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd against Linda Kao Chai-Chau; Linda sought leave to serve a third party notice against the Estate of Peter Fong for indemnity and contribution.
fact_changes.evidence: The source states that Suit 477/2012 is a derivative action brought by Carolyn Fong on behalf of AT against Linda, and that Linda applied for leave to serve a third party notice against PF's estate seeking indemnity and contribution. The current wording adds outcome-oriented and reasoning-heavy material.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Keep only the procedural background explicitly stated in the source.

fact_changes.current_value: The judgment analyses whether illegality bars claims for contribution or indemnity, the statutory basis for contribution under sections 15, 16 and 19 of the Civil Law Act, and whether third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong are redundant given Linda Kao Chai-Chau’s defence. The court discusses authorities including Burrows v Rhodes and Jameson, K v P, Nganthavee, Yemen, Westcoast, and Panweld, and concludes that statutory contribution is not barred by illegality and that the third party notices are not redundant. The court reasons that there are possible scenarios in which Linda could be found liable to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd while the estate of Peter Fong is also liable in respect of the same damage, justifying third party proceedings. The court allows Registrar's Appeals No 246 of 2013 and No 247 of 2013 and grants the applications to amend the third party notices, leaving questions of contribution apportionment and any discretionary exemption to the trial court after full evidence is heard.
fact_changes.evidence: This field contains court reasoning, authorities, and the judgment outcome, all of which are beyond the temporal wall for a predictive row.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the entire reasoning log.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-09-02, parties attended a hearing before the High Court on Registrar's Appeals No 246 of 2013 and No 247 of 2013 concerning third party proceedings related to Suit No 477 of 2012 and Suit No 1015 of 2012.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Hearing dates and hearing activity are litigation-stage material and fall beyond the temporal wall.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-10-11, parties made further submissions before the High Court on questions concerning the legal consequences of a director following instructions of a controlling chairman in relation to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Further submissions are litigation-stage material and the wording reflects court issues and argument.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-11-04, an application by Carolyn Fong for leave to hand over conduct of the derivative action in Suit No 477 of 2012 to the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd was heard, with the Receivers and Managers taking a neutral position.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is a procedural application and hearing-stage fact occurring after the dispute had moved into litigation.

rule_changes.note: Normalized unsupported date format to blank.
Normalized unsupported date format to blank.

case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit
party: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd (AT) – Suit No 1015 of 2012 (AT (RM))

audit_suggestion.case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit
audit_suggestion.party: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd (AT) – Suit No 1015 of 2012 (AT (RM))

fact_changes.current_value: Suit No 1015 of 2012; Registrar's Appeal No 247 of 2013; Summons No 4419 of 2013; Summons No 2989 of 2013
fact_changes.recommended_value: Suit No 1015 of 2012 (Registrar's Appeal No 247 of 2013; Summons No 4419 of 2013) and Suit No 477 of 2012 (Registrar's Appeal No 246 of 2013; Summons No 4420 of 2013)
fact_changes.evidence: The source states: "Case Number: Suit No 477 of 2012 (Registrar's Appeal No 246 of 2013; Summons No 4420 of 2013) and Suit No 1015 of 2012 (Registrar's Appeal No 247 of 2013; Summons No 4419 of 2013)".
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the case number string with the full case number as stated in the judgment.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says only that Suit No 1015 of 2012 "is brought by the Receivers and Managers ("RMs") of AT" and does not give a date for the commencement of the suit in the excerpt provided.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the invented date; keep only the fact that Suit No 1015 of 2012 was brought by the RMs of AT.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states: "After S 477/2012 was commenced, Ernst & Young was appointed RMs of AT on 17 January 2012."
fact_changes.recommended_change: No date change needed; however, the fact should be framed as the appointment of Ernst & Young as RMs of AT, not as a generic corporate role entry.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says only that "A consent order was subsequently granted pursuant to an apparent agreement between Carolyn and Linda" after the appointment of Ernst & Young as RMs. No exact date is given in the excerpt.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the invented exact date; preserve only the neutral fact that a consent order was subsequently granted.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that after their appointment, the RMs took the view that there was evidence of diversions and that these largely concern transactions or events occurring after PF's death, but it does not give a specific date for this review or view.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the invented date; keep only the neutral background that the RMs took the view there was evidence of diversions after their appointment.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says the RMs commenced S 1015/2012, but does not provide a specific commencement date in the excerpt.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the invented date; keep only that the RMs commenced Suit No 1015 of 2012.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that Linda commenced third party proceedings against the Estate of PF on 19 April 2013, but this is litigation-stage procedural material and should not be retained for a party row under the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove this litigation-stage procedural fact.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that on 12 June 2013 the RMs applied to set aside the third party proceedings, which is procedural litigation-stage material and should be removed under the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove this litigation-stage procedural fact.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that the Assistant Registrar delivered an oral decision on 19 July 2013, which is procedural/judgment-stage material and should be removed under the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove this litigation-stage procedural fact.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that on 26 August 2013 an application was made to amend the third party notice in Suit No 477 of 2012, which is litigation-stage procedural material and should be removed under the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove this litigation-stage procedural fact.

fact_changes.current_value: Whether, in Suit No 1015 of 2012, losses to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd from diversions of business after Peter Fong's death can be attributed solely to the conduct of Linda Kao Chai-Chau and other defendants, or also to conduct attributable to Peter Fong such that contribution or indemnity between them is in issue.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Suit No 1015 of 2012 is brought by the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd against Linda Kao Chai-Chau and 15 other individuals, including corporate individuals, concerning alleged diversions of business away from AT, largely involving events after Peter Fong's death.
fact_changes.evidence: The source states that S 1015/2012 "is brought by the Receivers and Managers ("RMs") of AT" and that after their appointment the RMs took the view there was evidence of diversions, "largely concern[ing] transactions or events occurring after the death of PF."
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the outcome-framed issue with a neutral background description of the suit.

fact_changes.current_value: Where more than one person is legally responsible in respect of the same damage suffered by a company, statutory provisions on contribution allow one responsible person to seek contribution from another, and procedural rules permit third party proceedings to resolve related questions in the same action.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Suit No 1015 of 2012 is a claim by the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd against Linda Kao Chai-Chau and others concerning alleged diversions of business away from AT.
fact_changes.evidence: The source's neutral background for the RM Action is that it was brought by the RMs against Linda and 15 others, including corporate individuals, and concerned alleged diversions of business away from AT.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove legal-rule and contribution framing; retain only neutral case background.

fact_changes.current_value: In Suit No 1015 of 2012, the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd pursue claims concerning transactions mainly after Peter Fong's death. Linda Kao Chai-Chau seeks to involve the estate of Peter Fong through third party proceedings for potential contribution or indemnity in respect of any overlapping loss to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd. The statutory contribution framework and third party procedure are used to align the resolution of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd's claims with any allocation of responsibility between Linda Kao Chai-Chau and the estate of Peter Fong.
fact_changes.recommended_value: In Suit No 1015 of 2012, the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd brought claims against Linda Kao Chai-Chau and others concerning alleged diversions of business away from AT, largely involving events after Peter Fong's death.
fact_changes.evidence: The source describes the RM Action as brought by the RMs against Linda and 15 others, with allegations mainly concerning events after PF's death.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove third-party, contribution, and allocation-of-responsibility material; keep only neutral background.

fact_changes.current_value: The court considers whether the third party proceedings in Suit No 1015 of 2012 are redundant or unnecessary, given Linda Kao Chai-Chau’s defence that she acted on Peter Fong’s instructions. It examines whether there are scenarios where Linda could be held responsible to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd while the estate of Peter Fong is also responsible for the same damage, justifying contribution. The court concludes that such scenarios are not fanciful and that the third party proceedings should proceed so that the trial court can determine, after hearing evidence, whether both Linda and Peter Fong’s estate are responsible in respect of the same damage and how any contribution should be apportioned. The court allows Registrar's Appeal No 247 of 2013 and the related application to amend the third party notice, leaving the exercise of the contribution discretion to the trial court.
fact_changes.evidence: This is judgment reasoning and outcome-stage material, including the court's conclusion and allowance of the appeal.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the entire judicial reasoning log.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-04-19, in Suit No 1015 of 2012, Linda Kao Chai-Chau commenced third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity in respect of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd's claims.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural litigation-stage fact; the source states this occurred on 19 April 2013.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-06-12, the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd applied by Summons No 2989 of 2013 to set aside the third party proceedings that Linda Kao Chai-Chau had commenced against the estate of Peter Fong in Suit No 1015 of 2012.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural litigation-stage fact; the source states this occurred on 12 June 2013.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-07-19, the Assistant Registrar delivered an oral decision on applications concerning third party proceedings in Suit No 1015 of 2012 and Suit No 477 of 2012.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural/judgment-stage fact; the source states this occurred on 19 July 2013.

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-08-26, an application was made to amend the third party notice in Suit No 477 of 2012 to add an alternative claim for contribution and to state that Peter Fong, as a director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, owed the same duties as Linda Kao Chai-Chau.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural litigation-stage fact; the source states this occurred on 26 August 2013.

facts_to_remove.current_value: The court considers whether the third party proceedings in Suit No 1015 of 2012 are redundant or unnecessary, given Linda Kao Chai-Chau’s defence that she acted on Peter Fong’s instructions. It examines whether there are scenarios where Linda could be held responsible to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd while the estate of Peter Fong is also responsible for the same damage, justifying contribution. The court concludes that such scenarios are not fanciful and that the third party proceedings should proceed so that the trial court can determine, after hearing evidence, whether both Linda and Peter Fong’s estate are responsible in respect of the same damage and how any contribution should be apportioned. The court allows Registrar's Appeal No 247 of 2013 and the related application to amend the third party notice, leaving the exercise of the contribution discretion to the trial court.

case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

audit_suggestion.case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

fact_changes.current_value: In 1996, Peter Fong was the majority shareholder and Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd when Linda Kao Chai-Chau was appointed as managing director.
fact_changes.recommended_value: In 1996, Peter Fong was the majority shareholder and Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, and Linda Kao Chai-Chau was appointed as managing director.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states that Linda was appointed as managing director of AT some time in 1996 by its majority shareholder and then Chairman, Peter Fong.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the implied simultaneity/causal wording 'when' and state the facts separately.

fact_changes.current_value: Around 2006, Peter Fong transferred the majority of his shares in Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd to the Fong Foundation charitable trust, after which he held about 3.6% of the shares and the Fong Foundation held about 51%.
fact_changes.recommended_value: In 2006, Peter Fong transferred some 51% of the shares to the Fong Foundation charitable trust; at the date of his death, he held 3.6% of the shares and the Fong Foundation held some 51%.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment says: 'That was when it is said PF had transferred the majority of his shares to the Fong Foundation charitable trust' and later 'At the date of his death, the point is made that PF only held 3.6% of the shares while the Fong Foundation held some 51%.'
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace 'around 2006' with 'in 2006' and align the shareholding description to the judgment's wording.

fact_changes.current_value: On 2008-04-25, Peter Fong, then Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, passed away.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Peter Fong passed away on 25 April 2008.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states: 'PF passed away on 25 April 2008.'
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported 'then Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd' wording.

fact_changes.current_value: By 2013, the estate of Peter Fong was named as the intended third party in Suit No 477 of 2012 and as third party in Suit No 1015 of 2012 in relation to potential indemnity or contribution claims by Linda Kao Chai-Chau.
fact_changes.evidence: This describes litigation-stage third party proceedings and is part of the procedural history in the judgment, which lies beyond the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove litigation-stage procedural facts about third party proceedings.

fact_changes.current_value: Up to the time of the appeals in 2013, the estate of Peter Fong did not file applications to set aside the third party notices or to object to the commencement of third party proceedings in Suit No 477 of 2012 or Suit No 1015 of 2012.
fact_changes.evidence: This is procedural litigation conduct occurring during the appeals and is beyond the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove procedural litigation conduct.

facts_to_remove.current_value: Whether the estate of Peter Fong may bear responsibility, alongside Linda Kao Chai-Chau, in respect of any loss suffered by Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd from the transactions in issue, such that contribution or indemnity between them is available.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This frames the dispute in terms of liability and contribution as litigated in the judgment, which is outcome- and trial-stage material.

facts_to_remove.current_value: If more than one person is legally responsible in respect of the same damage suffered by a company, statutory contribution provisions allow one such person to seek contribution from another, and third party procedures enable these questions to be determined within the same proceedings.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is a legal rule distilled from the judgment and not a pre-dispute fact.

facts_to_remove.current_value: The estate of Peter Fong is joined or intended to be joined as a third party in Suit No 477 of 2012 and Suit No 1015 of 2012 so that, if both Peter Fong and Linda Kao Chai-Chau are ultimately found responsible in respect of the same loss to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, the allocation of that loss between them can be addressed through contribution or indemnity within those suits.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is litigation-stage application/outcome material and refers to what was sought in the proceedings.

facts_to_remove.current_value: The judgment notes that the estate of Peter Fong, though separately represented, did not participate in the applications to set aside or oppose the third party proceedings. The court considers whether, on the assumed facts, Peter Fong could be responsible in respect of the same damage as Linda Kao Chai-Chau, and concludes that this is a realistic possibility. The court reasons that if both the managing director and the Chairman caused loss to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd through the transactions in question, statutory contribution between them may arise. The court therefore permits the third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong to continue and allows amendments to the third party notices, leaving the determination of any responsibility of the estate and any contribution to the trial court after full evidence is heard.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is entirely judgment reasoning and outcome-stage material, which must not cross the Temporal Wall.
facts_to_remove.recommended_change: Remove all judicial reasoning and outcome-stage content.

fact_changes.from: In 1996, Peter Fong was the majority shareholder and Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd when Linda Kao Chai-Chau was appointed as managing director.
fact_changes.to: In 1996, Peter Fong was the majority shareholder and Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, and Linda Kao Chai-Chau was appointed as managing director.

fact_changes.from: Around 2006, Peter Fong transferred the majority of his shares in Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd to the Fong Foundation charitable trust, after which he held about 3.6% of the shares and the Fong Foundation held about 51%.
fact_changes.to: In 2006, Peter Fong transferred some 51% of the shares to the Fong Foundation charitable trust; at the date of his death, he held 3.6% of the shares and the Fong Foundation held some 51%.

fact_changes.from: On 2008-04-25, Peter Fong, then Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, passed away.
fact_changes.to: Peter Fong passed away on 25 April 2008.

fact_changes.from: By 2013, the estate of Peter Fong was named as the intended third party in Suit No 477 of 2012 and as third party in Suit No 1015 of 2012 in relation to potential indemnity or contribution claims by Linda Kao Chai-Chau.

Up to the time of the appeals in 2013, the estate of Peter Fong did not file applications to set aside the third party notices or to object to the commencement of third party proceedings in Suit No 477 of 2012 or Suit No 1015 of 2012.

rule_changes.note: Removed fact with null or empty Text.
